beats.text: A student walks through a forest. Her eye detects light — that's our starting point.
Zoom into the eye. It's an organ. Inside sits the retina — a sheet of tissue lining the back wall.
Deeper now. A single photoreceptor cell within that retina. One cell, catching one photon.
Inside the cell, a mitochondrion appears — a dense, folded organelle floating in the cytoplasm.
The mitochondrion outputs a single molecule: ATP. A label locks onto it — ATP, the cell's energy currency.
Pull back through every level: molecule, organelle, cell, tissue, organ. Five layers. One student. One forest.
